project.name: next-clean
project.description: Open-source Next.js 16 starter template (App Router + Turbopack, React 19, TypeScript 5 strict, Tailwind CSS v4, Shadcn-style setup) with a type-safe Axios HTTP layer, TanStack Query data hooks, Zustand stores, t3-env validation, and SEO/JSON-LD metadata.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router API route handler exposing GET /api/v1/products, returning a Zod-validated array of products as a JSON response.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Next.js Route Handler convention (exported HTTP-method function) with a Zod schema to parse/validate the response payload.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: GET route handler that validates mock product data against the product schema and returns it via Response.json.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the products API layer (query-key factory and raw API functions) for the products module.

nodes.name: product-query-keys.ts
nodes.summary: Hierarchical TanStack Query key factory for the products feature; all keys descend from a shared root so broad invalidation cascades to every list and detail entry.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `as const` tuples to give each query key a precise readonly type, enabling type-safe cache invalidation.

nodes.name: use-product-mutation.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query mutation hooks for creating, updating, and deleting products, each invalidating the relevant query-key slices on success.

nodes.name: useCreateProduct
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that creates a product and invalidates the product lists cache on success.

nodes.name: useUpdateProduct
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that updates a product and invalidates both the product lists and the affected detail cache entry on success.

nodes.name: useDeleteProduct
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that deletes a product and invalidates the product lists cache on success.

nodes.name: use-products.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query read hooks for fetching the product list (with optional filters) and a single product by id, keyed via the product query-key factory.

nodes.name: useProducts
nodes.summary: Query hook that fetches the filtered product list via getProducts, keyed by the list query key.

nodes.name: useProduct
nodes.summary: Query hook that fetches a single product by id via getProduct, keyed by the detail query key.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the product domain types and the Zod productSchema used for runtime validation across the products module and API route.
nodes.languageNotes: Co-locates a Zod schema with inferred TypeScript types, giving a single source of truth for both runtime validation and static typing.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file for the constants layer, re-exporting env, API endpoints, enums, and regex modules so consumers import via the single ~/constants entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel using re-exports to expose the constants directory as one module.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file for the HTTP layer, re-exporting the apiRequest client wrapper (and related API helpers) under the ~/lib/api entry point.

nodes.name: product-api.ts
nodes.summary: Raw product API functions for the products feature module, wrapping apiRequest to perform CRUD operations against the products endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin typed wrappers delegating to the generic apiRequest<T> client with strongly-typed Product return shapes.

nodes.name: url.ts
nodes.summary: String utility that rewrites raw URLs in text into external anchor tags with configurable styling.

nodes.name: email.ts
nodes.summary: Validation helper that checks whether a string is a well-formed email using the shared EMAIL_VALIDATION regex.

nodes.name: getProducts
nodes.summary: Fetches the product list via a GET request, forwarding optional filters as query params and returning a typed Product array.

nodes.name: getProduct
nodes.summary: Fetches a single product by id via a GET request to the products endpoint.

nodes.name: createProduct
nodes.summary: Creates a new product via a POST request, sending the payload (Product without id) and returning the created Product.

nodes.name: updateProduct
nodes.summary: Updates an existing product via a PATCH request, extracting the id for the URL and sending the remaining fields as the body.

nodes.name: deleteProduct
nodes.summary: Deletes a product by id via a DELETE request to the products endpoint.

nodes.name: makeUrlsExternal
nodes.summary: Replaces raw URLs found in a text string with external anchor tags (target/rel attributes) and an optional CSS class.

nodes.name: isValidEmail
nodes.summary: Returns true when the given string matches the shared EMAIL_VALIDATION regex pattern.

nodes.name: error.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router global error boundary that renders a styled fallback UI, logs the error via useEffect, formats the error message with makeUrlsExternal, and offers a reset action plus a home link.
nodes.languageNotes: App Router special file requiring 'use client' and the ({ error, reset }) prop contract.

nodes.name: ErrorPage
nodes.summary: Client error boundary component that logs the caught error and renders a recovery screen with a background pattern, formatted message, retry button, and home navigation link.

nodes.name: not-found.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router 404 page that renders a not-found screen using a shared background pattern from the components barrel.
nodes.languageNotes: App Router special file (not-found.tsx) rendered for unmatched routes and notFound() calls.

nodes.name: NotFoundPage
nodes.summary: Renders the 404 not-found UI including a shared background pattern.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the components directory so consumers import shared UI (e.g. getBackgroundPattern) via the ~/components alias.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the utils directory so consumers import pure helpers (e.g. cn, makeUrlsExternal) via the ~/utils alias.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router root layout that defines viewport and SEO metadata, injects JSON-LD structured data, and wraps all pages in the global RootProviders.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports Next.js metadata/viewport route conventions alongside the default layout component.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Root layout component rendering the html/body shell, composed classes via cn(), JSON-LD script tag, and the RootProviders wrapper around page children.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting UI primitive components from the components/ui directory for convenient import via the directory path.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Composes app-wide providers (ReactQueryProvider, nuqs adapter) via reduceRight and renders global components like the Sonner Toaster, exposing the RootProviders wrapper used in the root layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses reduceRight over a providers array to avoid deeply nested provider JSX.

nodes.name: RootProviders
nodes.summary: Wraps children in the configured provider stack via reduceRight and renders global components (Toaster) alongside the page content.

nodes.name: react-query.tsx
nodes.summary: Configures the TanStack Query QueryClient (staleTime, retry logic) and a QueryCache that surfaces errors as Sonner toasts, exposing the ReactQueryProvider used in the integrations stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Holds the QueryClient in useState so it is created once per client mount rather than per render.

nodes.name: ReactQueryProvider
nodes.summary: Provider component that lazily instantiates a configured QueryClient with global query/mutation error handling via toast notifications and wraps children in QueryClientProvider.

nodes.name: robot.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router robots metadata route that generates robots.txt rules from the app config, allowing all crawlers and pointing to the sitemap.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Next.js convention file `robots.ts` which exports a default function returning a MetadataRoute.Robots object.

nodes.name: robots
nodes.summary: Default-exported function returning robots.txt directives (crawler rules and sitemap URL) derived from the application domain config.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the config module's public surface (app config and SEO config).

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Exposes the generic, type-safe `apiRequest<T>` wrapper that all API calls use, delegating to the configured Axios instance and unwrapping the response payload.
nodes.languageNotes: Generic function typed via `ApiEndpoint`/`Method`; re-exports `ApiError` so consumers import error handling and the request helper from a single module.

nodes.name: apiRequest
nodes.summary: Generic async request handler that issues a typed Axios request for a given method/endpoint and returns the unwrapped response data; errors are normalized upstream by the response interceptor.

nodes.name: interceptor.ts
nodes.summary: Configured Axios instance (baseURL from appConfig.domainName) with request/response interceptors for auth headers and status-code handling, plus the `ApiError` class used to normalize failures.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralizes Axios configuration and normalizes thrown errors into a custom `ApiError` carrying HTTP status and code.

nodes.name: ApiError
nodes.summary: Custom Error subclass carrying HTTP status and error code, used to normalize API failures across the HTTP layer.

nodes.name: app.config.ts
nodes.summary: Exposes the central `appConfig` object holding application-wide settings such as app name, domain name, theme, and social links, consumed across the SEO and integration layers.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript const config object typed via the shared types barrel.

nodes.name: seo.config.ts
nodes.summary: Builds per-page SEO metadata via `generateSeoMetadata()` (titles, descriptions, OpenGraph, canonical URLs) and exports a `jsonLd` structured-data object, seeding defaults from `appConfig`.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Next.js `Metadata` type and schema-dts `WithContext<SoftwareApplication>` for typed JSON-LD.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for project-wide shared TypeScript types re-exported under `~/types`.

nodes.name: generateSeoMetadata
nodes.summary: Produces a Next.js `Metadata`/`SeoMeta` object, merging caller overrides with `appConfig` defaults for title, description, keywords, OpenGraph, and canonical URL; called in the root layout and overridable per page.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel for the lib/store layer, re-exporting the createStore Zustand factory so feature stores import it via the directory path.

nodes.name: cart-store.ts
nodes.summary: Client-only cart Zustand store built with createStore (immer, non-persisted), holding cart items plus add/update/remove actions and exposing granular selector hooks (useCart, useCartState, useCartActions).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses zustand/react/shallow useShallow to memoize object selectors and keep server-cache state (products via TanStack Query) separate from client cart state.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel for the products module, re-exporting its api, hooks, types, and store so consumers import the slice through ~/modules/products.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Entry-point definition for the codebase-design skill, establishing a shared vocabulary (module, interface, seam, depth, adapter, leverage, locality) and principles for designing deep modules with testable interfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Claude Code skill document with YAML frontmatter (name + description) followed by markdown body; links to companion sub-documents via relative paths.

nodes.name: DEEPENING.md
nodes.summary: Companion sub-document explaining how to deepen a module given its dependencies, covering the four dependency categories, seam discipline, and a replace-don't-layer testing strategy.

nodes.name: DESIGN-IT-TWICE.md
nodes.summary: Companion sub-document describing a process for exploring alternative module interfaces by spawning parallel sub-agents and comparing the designs on depth, locality, and seam placement.

nodes.name: .commitlintrc.json
nodes.summary: Commitlint configuration that extends @commitlint/config-conventional to enforce conventional commit message formats (feat:, chore:, etc.) via the Husky commit-msg hook.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template enumerating the environment variables the app expects, mirroring the t3-env zod schema in src/constants/env.ts so developers can scaffold their own .env file.

nodes.name: .prettierrc.json
nodes.summary: Prettier formatting configuration defining the repo's code style: no semicolons, single quotes, 3-space tabs, 120 print width, trailing commas, and avoid-style arrow parens.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Guidance document for Claude Code describing the project stack, commands, conventions, and the module-based architecture (modules, lib/api, lib/store, integrations, config).

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Contributor Covenant code of conduct outlining community standards, enforcement responsibilities, and a tiered enforcement guideline for the open-source project.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Domain glossary defining core project concepts and the self-contained module slice pattern (api, hooks, store, types, barrel) used under src/modules.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project landing documentation introducing the Next.js starter template, listing its key features (App Router, ESLint, Tailwind, t3-env, type-safe Axios, Husky), and getting-started instructions.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Security policy describing supported versions and the process for privately reporting vulnerabilities in the project.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Project manifest declaring dependencies (Next.js 16, React 19, TanStack Query, Zustand, Axios, t3-env), yarn scripts (dev, build, typecheck, lint, format), and lint-staged/commit tooling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses overrides and resolutions to pin transitive dependency versions; type: module enables ESM.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lockfile pinning the versions and sources of the Claude Code skills installed in this repository for reproducible tooling.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode and defining the ~/* to src/* path alias used for all internal imports, plus include/exclude globs for the build.
nodes.languageNotes: Path mapping (~/* -> src/*) drives the project's import convention enforced by ESLint simple-import-sort.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Root Tailwind CSS v4 stylesheet that imports tailwindcss and the layered defaults/utilities/elements partials, then defines the CSS-first @theme with the project's oklch brand colors and font-sans token.
nodes.languageNotes: Tailwind v4 CSS-first config: uses @import 'tailwindcss', cascade layers via layer(base|utilities|components), @theme/@theme inline blocks, and oklch() color definitions instead of a tailwind.config.js.

nodes.name: defaults.css
nodes.summary: Base-layer reset partial that applies a default border-color to all elements and pseudo-elements, restoring Tailwind's preflight border behavior.

nodes.name: elements.css
nodes.summary: Components-layer partial defining the reusable .card class styled with the theme's deep-brown color, rounded corners, spacing, and shadow tokens.

nodes.name: utils.css
nodes.summary: Empty utilities-layer partial reserved for project-specific custom utility classes, imported into index.css under layer(utilities).

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code skill definition describing a documentation-grilling workflow for adversarially questioning docs.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code skill definition describing a session handoff workflow for transferring task context.

nodes.name: HTML-REPORT.md
nodes.summary: Reference guide for generating HTML architecture reports, covering scaffold, candidate cards, Mermaid diagram patterns, and styling conventions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code skill defining a three-step process (explore, present HTML candidates, grilling loop) for improving codebase architecture.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code skill describing Obsidian vault management workflows including note search, creation, linking, and index discovery.

nodes.name: commit-msg
nodes.summary: Husky Git hook that runs commitlint to enforce conventional commit message format.

nodes.name: pre-commit
nodes.summary: Husky Git hook that runs lint-staged to lint and format staged files before commit.

nodes.name: .prettierignore
nodes.summary: Lists paths excluded from Prettier formatting.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file controlling which paths the Understand-Anything analyzer skips when scanning the project.

nodes.name: commitlint.config.js
nodes.summary: Commitlint configuration extending @commitlint/config-conventional to enforce conventional commit messages.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: Flat ESLint configuration wiring up Next.js, TypeScript, simple-import-sort, and inline type-import rules for the project.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the ESLint flat config (.mjs) format composing multiple shared rule sets.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration that validates environment variables at build time by importing env.ts through jiti, and enables project-wide build options.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports the TS env schema via jiti so invalid environment variables fail the build.

nodes.name: postcss.config.cjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the Tailwind CSS v4 plugin for the build pipeline.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Layout component for the (auth) route group wrapping sign-in and sign-up pages.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Sign-in page route component within the (auth) route group.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Sign-up page route component within the (auth) route group.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Marketing home page client component showing a copyable git clone command with a click-to-copy counter and toast feedback.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Placeholder file keeping the empty layout components directory under version control.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Placeholder file keeping the empty shared components directory under version control.

nodes.name: bg-patterns.tsx
nodes.summary: Catalog of decorative background pattern components (grids and gradients, light/dark variants) plus a BACKGROUND_OPTIONS registry and getBackgroundPattern lookup helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines many small presentational components keyed into a single options array for runtime lookup.

nodes.name: fonts.ts
nodes.summary: Configures and exports the Inter web font via next/font for use across the application.

nodes.name: api-endpoints.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the API_ENDPOINTS constant and the ApiRouteKeys type used to type-check endpoint references in the HTTP layer.

nodes.name: enums.ts
nodes.summary: Shared enumeration constants used across the application.

nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: Validates and exports typed environment variables using t3-env and zod, splitting server and client schemas with Vercel preset extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses t3-env createEnv with zod schemas; client vars are NEXT_PUBLIC_-prefixed and validation runs at build time.

nodes.name: regex.ts
nodes.summary: Central collection of reusable regular expression patterns (the REGEX constant) for validation across the project.

nodes.name: getBackgroundPattern
nodes.summary: Looks up a background pattern component by id from the BACKGROUND_OPTIONS registry.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the app-wide React hooks (use-copy, use-toggle, use-mouse-position, use-isomorphic-layout) so they are reachable via the ~/hooks directory import.

nodes.name: use-copy.ts
nodes.summary: App-wide React hook that copies text to the clipboard via the Clipboard API and tracks a transient copied state that auto-resets after a configurable delay.

nodes.name: use-isomorphic-layout.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny SSR-safe hook that resolves to useLayoutEffect on the client and useEffect on the server to avoid React hydration warnings.

nodes.name: use-mouse-position.ts
nodes.summary: App-wide React hook that tracks the mouse position relative to an optional ref element, using a layout effect to attach and clean up a document mousemove listener.

nodes.name: use-toggle.ts
nodes.summary: Small React hook exposing a boolean state plus a memoized toggle callback for on/off UI interactions.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file for the lib layer, re-exporting the API and store submodules for ~/lib directory imports.

nodes.name: create-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store factory wrapping a creator with immer (direct state mutation) and optional persist middleware; supports skipPersist and a custom localStorage name via config.
nodes.languageNotes: Generic createStore<T> composes immer and persist middleware, conditionally selecting the persisted vs non-persisted variant based on config.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file for the cart feature module, re-exporting its store slice.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the cart Zustand store and its selector hooks.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the products feature TanStack Query hooks (queries and mutations).

nodes.name: proxy.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js middleware that performs route redirects via NextResponse.redirect, with a config matcher controlling which paths it runs on.

nodes.name: config.types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions describing the application config shape (app metadata, domain, theme, socials).

nodes.name: cn.ts
nodes.summary: Exposes the cn() class-name helper that merges clsx conditional classes through tailwind-merge to resolve Tailwind conflicts.

nodes.name: currency.ts
nodes.summary: Currency formatting helper that renders a numeric amount as a USD-formatted string via Intl.NumberFormat.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the formatting utilities (e.g. currency formatting).

nodes.name: capitalization.ts
nodes.summary: String helper that capitalizes the first letter of each word in a lowercased input string.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the string utilities (capitalization, slugify, truncate).

nodes.name: slugify.ts
nodes.summary: String utilities producing URL-safe slugs: a basic slugify normalizing diacritics and separators, plus professionalSlugify applying custom term replacements before slugifying.

nodes.name: truncate.ts
nodes.summary: String truncation helpers: truncate cuts to a max character length with an ellipsis, and truncateWords avoids splitting mid-word.

nodes.name: uuid.ts
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper exposing generateUUID() that returns a v4 UUID via the uuid package.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the validation utilities.

nodes.name: useCopyToClipboard
nodes.summary: Hook that returns a copy callback and an isCopied flag; writes text to the clipboard and resets the copied state after a configurable delay.

nodes.name: useMousePosition
nodes.summary: Hook tracking the cursor position relative to an optional ref element, attaching a document mousemove listener inside a layout effect and cleaning it up on unmount.

nodes.name: useToggle
nodes.summary: Hook returning a boolean state and a memoized toggle callback that flips the value.

nodes.name: createStore
nodes.summary: Generic Zustand store factory that wraps the creator with immer and, unless skipPersist is set, persist + createJSONStorage middleware keyed by a configurable name.

nodes.name: middleware
nodes.summary: Next.js middleware entry that issues a redirect via NextResponse.redirect for matched requests.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges conditional class names via clsx and resolves Tailwind class conflicts with tailwind-merge.

nodes.name: formatToUSD
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric amount as a USD currency string using Intl.NumberFormat with the en-US locale.

nodes.name: pureCapitalize
nodes.summary: Lowercases the input and capitalizes the first character of each word via a regex replace.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Normalizes a string into a URL-safe slug by handling diacritics, lowercasing, trimming, and collapsing separators.

nodes.name: professionalSlugify
nodes.summary: Applies a map of custom term replacements to the input before delegating to slugify, producing a refined slug.

nodes.name: truncate
nodes.summary: Truncates text to a maximum character length, appending an ellipsis when shortened.

nodes.name: truncateWords
nodes.summary: Truncates text to a maximum length without splitting mid-word by cutting at the last space, then appends an ellipsis.

nodes.name: generateUUID
nodes.summary: Returns a v4 UUID string by delegating to the uuid package's uuidv4.

layers.name: App & Routing
layers.description: Next.js App Router entry points — route groups ((auth), (marketing)), the root layout/providers wiring, special files (error, not-found, robots), the /api/v1 route handler, and the redirect middleware.

layers.name: Feature Modules
layers.description: Self-contained feature slices under src/modules (products API/query-keys/hooks/types, cart Zustand store) that compose the core HTTP and store layers into domain functionality.

layers.name: Core Library & Integrations
layers.description: Cross-cutting infrastructure: the type-safe Axios HTTP layer (apiRequest client + interceptor/ApiError), the createStore Zustand factory, and the app-wide provider stack (TanStack Query, nuqs, Toaster).

layers.name: UI, Hooks & Styling
layers.description: Presentational building blocks: shared UI components (background patterns, fonts), the Tailwind v4 CSS-first theme and layered style partials, and reusable app-wide React interaction hooks (copy, toggle, mouse-position, isomorphic-layout).

layers.name: Foundation
layers.description: Project-wide primitives every other layer depends on: validated env/constants/enums/regex, app & SEO config, shared TypeScript types, and pure utility helpers (cn, uuid, string/formatting/validation).

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Markdown documentation for the template — README, CLAUDE/CONTEXT guidance, governance (CODE_OF_CONDUCT, SECURITY), and the bundled Claude Code skill definitions under .claude/skills.

layers.name: Tooling & Configuration
layers.description: Build, lint, format, and Git-hook configuration that governs the repo: package.json, tsconfig, ESLint/Prettier/PostCSS/commitlint configs, next.config, .env.example, Husky hooks, and ignore files.

tour.title: What Is This Project?
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the template's mission: a feature-packed, customizable Next.js 16 starter (App Router + Turbopack, React 19, strict TypeScript, Tailwind v4, SEO, env validation, type-safe Axios, Husky). CONTEXT.md and CLAUDE.md then translate that pitch into the actual conventions you'll see throughout the tour — the self-contained module-slice pattern, the ~/* path alias, and barrel exports. Read these first to get the map before walking the territory.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The root layout is where every page begins. It defines viewport and SEO metadata, injects JSON-LD structured data, mounts the global font and stylesheet, and wraps all routes in <RootProviders>. This single file is the spine of the app — the BFS dependency traversal starts here, and the rest of the tour follows the imports fanning out from it.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses convention-based 'special files'. layout.tsx exports a default component plus typed `metadata`/`viewport` objects that Next.js reads at build time to populate <head> — no manual <head> wiring needed.

tour.title: Booting the Provider Stack
tour.description: Building on the layout's <RootProviders> import, the integrations layer composes app-wide context providers. index.tsx flattens a providers array with reduceRight (avoiding deeply nested JSX) and renders globals like the Sonner Toaster, while react-query.tsx configures the TanStack Query client — 5-minute staleTime, retry logic, and a QueryCache that surfaces errors as toasts. This is how client-side data fetching and global UI get wired in exactly once.
tour.languageLesson: The QueryClient is held in useState(() => new QueryClient()) so it is instantiated once per client mount rather than recreated on every render — a standard React pattern for expensive, stable singletons.

tour.title: App & SEO Configuration
tour.description: The layout pulls its metadata from the config layer. app.config.ts is the single source of truth for app name, domain, theme, and social links, while seo.config.ts exposes generateSeoMetadata() — a factory that merges per-page overrides with those defaults to produce Next.js Metadata plus typed JSON-LD structured data. The root layout calls it once; any page can override it by exporting its own metadata.

tour.title: Validated Environment & Constants
tour.description: Beneath config sits the foundation layer. env.ts validates environment variables at build time with t3-env + zod (server and client schemas, NEXT_PUBLIC_-prefixed client vars), so a missing or malformed variable fails the build rather than crashing at runtime. Alongside it, api-endpoints.ts defines the typed route map that the HTTP layer keys against, with .env.example mirroring the schema so contributors can scaffold their own .env.
tour.languageLesson: next.config.ts imports env.ts through jiti so the zod schema runs during the build. Combined with t3-env's createEnv, invalid environment variables become a compile-time error instead of a production surprise.

tour.title: The Type-Safe HTTP Layer
tour.description: This is the most depended-upon code in the project — apiRequest has the highest fan-in in the whole graph. interceptor.ts is the configured Axios instance: baseURL from appConfig.domainName, a request interceptor that attaches auth headers, a response interceptor that normalizes failures into a custom ApiError carrying HTTP status and code. client.ts wraps it in the generic apiRequest<T>(method, endpoint, data?) that every API call in the app flows through.
tour.languageLesson: apiRequest<T> is a generic function: the caller specifies the expected response shape T, and TypeScript propagates it through the Axios call so each call site gets a strongly-typed result without casting.

tour.title: Feature Slice — Types & Raw API
tour.description: Now we descend into a real feature module. The products slice begins with its data model: types.ts co-locates a Zod productSchema with the TypeScript types inferred from it, giving one source of truth for both runtime validation and static typing (it's the second-highest fan-in node). product-api.ts then provides thin, typed CRUD wrappers (getProducts, getProduct, createProduct, updateProduct, deleteProduct) that all delegate to apiRequest from Step 6.
tour.languageLesson: Zod's z.infer<typeof productSchema> derives the static type directly from the validation schema, so the runtime guard and the compile-time type can never drift apart.

tour.title: Feature Slice — Query Keys & Hooks
tour.description: On top of the raw API sits the TanStack Query layer. product-query-keys.ts is a hierarchical key factory where every key descends from a shared root, so a single invalidation can cascade to all lists and details. use-products.ts exposes read hooks (useProducts list, useProduct detail) and use-product-mutation.ts exposes create/update/delete mutations that invalidate the right key slices on success — closing the component-to-server loop.
tour.languageLesson: Query keys are declared with `as const` tuples, giving each key a precise readonly type. This lets TanStack Query match keys structurally for cache invalidation while TypeScript guards against typos.

tour.title: The Server Side of the Slice
tour.description: The products feature is end-to-end: the same Zod schema from Step 7 also guards the server. This App Router route handler exposes GET /api/v1/products, validating mock product data against productSchema before returning it as JSON. It demonstrates the template's full data round-trip — one schema validating both the response the server emits and the data the client hooks consume.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js Route Handlers export functions named after HTTP methods (GET, POST, ...). The exported `GET` here returns a Response via Response.json, the App Router equivalent of the old pages/api handlers.

tour.title: Client State — The Store Factory
tour.description: Where server state lives in TanStack Query, client/UI state lives in Zustand. create-store.ts is a generic createStore<T> factory wrapping Zustand with immer (mutate state directly inside set) and persist middleware, with a skipPersist flag for ephemeral stores. cart-store.ts applies it: a non-persisted cart store with add/update/remove actions exposing granular selector hooks (useCart, useCartActions) rather than the whole store, keeping client cart state cleanly separate from server-cached products.
tour.languageLesson: Selectors use zustand/react/shallow's useShallow to memoize object selections, so a component re-renders only when the specific slice it reads actually changes — not on every store update.

tour.title: Styling — Tailwind v4 Theme
tour.description: Returning to a depth-1 import from the root layout, the styling system is Tailwind CSS v4 with CSS-first config — no tailwind.config.js. index.css imports tailwindcss and the layered defaults/utils/elements partials, then defines an @theme block of oklch brand colors and the font token. The cn() helper (clsx + tailwind-merge) is the companion used everywhere to compose conditional classes and resolve Tailwind conflicts.
tour.languageLesson: Tailwind v4 moves configuration into CSS: @import 'tailwindcss', cascade layers via @layer, and @theme blocks using oklch() colors replace the JS config file entirely.

tour.title: Shared UI, Hooks & Error States
tour.description: The presentational toolkit rounds out the front end. bg-patterns.tsx is a registry of decorative background components with a getBackgroundPattern lookup, consumed by the App Router's special error.tsx and not-found.tsx screens. The app-wide hooks barrel collects reusable interaction logic — useCopyToClipboard, useToggle, useMousePosition, and an SSR-safe isomorphic layout effect — the kind of primitives the marketing page's click-to-copy demo is built from.
tour.languageLesson: error.tsx and not-found.tsx are App Router special files. error.tsx must be a 'use client' component and receives the ({ error, reset }) prop contract, letting it both display and recover from runtime errors.

tour.title: Pure Utilities & Routing Glue
tour.description: At the foundation are pure, dependency-light helpers: string utilities (slugify, truncate, capitalize, makeUrlsExternal), formatting (formatToUSD), validation (isValidEmail backed by shared regex), and generateUUID. proxy.ts is the Next.js middleware that handles route redirects via a config matcher. These are the leaf nodes everything else quietly leans on.

tour.title: Tooling, Quality & Conventions
tour.description: Finally, the guardrails that keep contributions consistent. package.json declares the dependency set and yarn scripts (dev, build, typecheck, lint); tsconfig.json enables strict mode and the ~/* path alias; eslint.config.mjs enforces import sorting and inline type imports; .prettierrc.json fixes the house style (no semicolons, single quotes, 3-space tabs). The Husky pre-commit and commit-msg hooks run lint-staged and commitlint, so formatting and conventional-commit messages are enforced before code ever lands.
tour.languageLesson: Conventional commits (feat:, chore:, fix:) are enforced by commitlint via the Husky commit-msg hook. Paired with lint-staged in pre-commit, the repo guarantees every commit is formatted, linted, and conventionally named.
